note: Oscillating resolution (not converging) + score all tiles + sort. Measures language overhead on iteration-heavy workload.

nodejs.note: V8 array + sort fast, 2x slower than Go

rust.note: Vec.sort fast, but score() iteration overhead higher

python.note: List comprehension + sort slowest by far

key_finding: Python is 8.6x slower than Go here. The oscillating pattern (continuous scoring every tick) amplifies language overhead vs the deposit-only workload. For high-frequency room heartbeats, Python can't keep up.
production_implication: AbstractionRoom with 1000 tiles at 10Hz heartbeat needs 10K scores/sec. Python at 806 = can handle 80 rooms. Go at 6951 = can handle 695 rooms. At fleet scale (hundreds of rooms), only Go/Rust can sustain the tick rate.
